homepage.heroSubline: Protecting your home from Texas storms since 2012. Locally owned & committed to quality craftsmanship for every roof we touch.
homepage.aboutTitle: Rooted in Austin, Built on Trust
aboutText: Founded in 2012, Apex Roofing & Restoration started as a small crew servicing historic homes in Travis County. Today, we're proud to serve growing communities across Williamson County with the same attention to detail.
We approach every project like it's our own home—using premium materials suited for Central Texas weather and training our team in storm damage diagnostics specific to our region.
When we're not on rooftops, you'll find us volunteering with Austin Habitat for Humanity or sponsoring Little League teams in Round Rock.

valueProps.title: Weather-Tested Materials
valueProps.description: We specify impact-resistant shingles and fortified underlayment to withstand Austin's hailstorms and 100°F summers.

valueProps.title: Storm Damage Specialists
valueProps.description: Trained to identify subtle wind and hail damage that Austin insurers frequently miss during claims.

valueProps.title: Local Knowledge
valueProps.description: Understands how limestone soils and flash floods affect roofs differently in Barton Hills vs. new Pflugerville builds.

valueProps.title: Transparent Pricing
valueProps.description: Detailed quotes with line-item breakdowns—no surprise fees after we start work.

homepage.ctaSubline: Our Austin-based team responds within 24 hours—even after major storms.

services.name: Roof Replacement
services.shortDescription: Complete roof overhauls designed for Austin's extreme weather cycles.
services.description:
When those 110°F summers bake your shingles or a hailstorm leaves pockmarks across your roof, replacement becomes inevitable. We specialize in full tear-offs that address underlying decking issues common in older Austin homes.

Our process starts with inspecting for moisture damage in the plywood—especially crucial for bungalows near Lady Bird Lake where humidity lingers. For newer subdivisions in Cedar Park, we focus on proper ventilation to prevent premature aging from attic heat buildup.

Choose from Class 4 impact-resistant shingles that meet Austin's strict hail ratings, or opt for energy-reflective metal roofs that slash cooling costs during our relentless summers. Every replacement includes upgraded ice/water shield at the eaves—a must for Central Texas' sudden downpours.
process: Step 1: Comprehensive inspection including moisture meter readings and attic airflow assessment
Step 2: Customized material selection based on your neighborhood's hail history and sun exposure
Step 3: Full tear-off with debris containment (we recycle 90% of materials)
Step 4: Installation with focus on leak-prone areas like chimney flashings
benefits: Energy Star-rated options reduce AC strain during Austin summers
5-year workmanship warranty backed by local availability
Storm-ready designs that meet Travis County building codes
Preservation of historic home character in central neighborhoods

faq.question: How long does a roof replacement take in Austin?
faq.answer: Most homes take 2-3 days, but we allow extra time for historic properties where we may encounter unexpected decking repairs. Weather delays are rare—we plan around rain forecasts.

faq.question: Will you handle HOA approvals in master-planned communities?
faq.answer: Yes, we're familiar with design guidelines in Circle C Ranch and similar neighborhoods, providing all required documentation for architectural review boards.

services.name: Storm Damage Repair
services.shortDescription: Emergency repairs and insurance claim support after Austin hailstorms.
services.description:
Central Texas sits in 'Hail Alley,' where baseball-sized ice chunks can pummel roofs in minutes. Our team deploys within hours of major storms to perform temporary tarping—especially critical for older homes in East Austin with original wood shake roofs.

We document damage with 360° photo reports that insurers like State Farm and USAA routinely accept, noting subtle granule loss that indicates future leaks. For homes near the Williamson County line, we check for foundation-related roof stress from our clay-rich soils.

Unlike storm chasers who disappear after claims, we remain your permanent repair partner. Our fixes blend seamlessly with existing materials, whether matching historic slate tiles downtown or color-blending composite shingles in Mueller's modern builds.
process: Step 1: Emergency tarping within 12 hours for active leaks
Step 2: Detailed damage report with measurements and close-up photos
Step 3: Insurance claim assistance with preferred local adjusters
Step 4: Permanent repairs using matching materials
benefits: 24/7 response team based in Round Rock
No upfront payments—we work directly with your insurer
Granule loss analysis predicts future failure points
Familiarity with Travis County's most common storm damage patterns

faq.question: How soon should I get my roof inspected after a storm?
faq.answer: Within 72 hours is ideal—wind can dislodge flashing that appears intact, and early documentation strengthens insurance claims. We offer free post-storm evaluations.

faq.question: Do you repair metal roofs damaged by hail?
faq.answer: Absolutely. We have specialized tools to reshape dented metal panels on commercial properties and modern homes, often avoiding full replacement.

services.name: Commercial Roofing
services.shortDescription: Durable solutions for Austin offices, retail centers, and mixed-use buildings.
services.description:
Austin's booming commercial sector needs roofs that withstand delivery truck impacts, rooftop HVAC units, and the occasional downtown fireworks display. We specialize in flat roof systems for South Congress boutique hotels and standing seam metal for tech campuses in the Domain.

Our crews are OSHA-certified to work around sensitive equipment at medical offices and data centers. For restaurants near Rainey Street, we install grease-resistant membranes that won't degrade from kitchen exhaust. Every project includes after-hours options to minimize business disruption.

We monitor Williamson County's unique weather patterns—designing drainage for the 6" rainfalls that flood Pflugerville parking lots, and specifying UV-resistant coatings for west-facing retail strips in Cedar Park.
process: Step 1: Site evaluation focusing on foot traffic patterns and equipment loads
Step 2: Material selection based on your industry's specific needs
Step 3: Phased installation coordinated with your operations schedule
Step 4: Semi-annual maintenance plans with priority response
benefits: Class A fire-rated systems for multi-tenant buildings
Energy-efficient TPO membranes reduce cooling costs
Custom flashing solutions for rooftop patios and signage
Compliance with Austin's commercial building codes

faq.question: What's the lifespan of a typical Austin commercial roof?
faq.answer: 15-25 years depending on material, but our maintenance program can extend that by addressing minor issues before they become major repairs—especially important with Austin's extreme temperature swings.

faq.question: Can you work around our business hours?
faq.answer: Yes, we routinely schedule weekend and overnight work for retailers and restaurants, using temporary noise barriers when needed.

services.name: Gutter Installation
services.shortDescription: Heavy-duty gutters that handle Austin's torrential downpours.
services.description:
Austin's 'Flash Flood Alley' demands gutters that move water away from foundations quickly. We install 6" commercial-grade aluminum systems—wider than standard residential models—to prevent overflow during those sudden 3-inch rains.

For historic homes in Clarksville, we craft seamless half-round gutters that match original profiles. Newer Georgetown builds get our leaf-guard systems with extra downspouts to account for live oak debris. Every installation includes soil grading checks to ensure proper drainage away from Central Texas' problematic clay soils.

We don't just slap on gutters—we engineer complete water management systems. This means adding rain chains for bungalow charm in Travis Heights, or discreet underground drains for minimalist modern homes in West Lake Hills.
process: Step 1: Water flow analysis using your roof's pitch and square footage
Step 2: Custom fabrication on-site for perfect fit (no prefab sections)
Step 3: Reinforcement at stress points where Austin's heavy rains concentrate
Step 4: Downspout placement that directs water away from foundations
benefits: Seamless construction eliminates leak-prone joints
Heavy-duty hangers withstand 70+ mph wind gusts
Color matching for historic district compliance
Slope calculations prevent standing water breeding mosquitoes

faq.question: How often should Austin gutters be cleaned?
faq.Answer: Twice annually—after spring oak pollen drops and again in fall. We offer maintenance plans that include debris removal and sealant checks.

faq.question: Can gutters prevent foundation issues in our clay soil?
faq.Answer: Properly installed gutters are your first defense. We extend downspouts 5+ feet from foundations in neighborhoods like Circle C where soil movement is common.

services.name: Roof Inspection
services.shortDescription: Thorough evaluations that spot Austin-specific roof vulnerabilities.
services.description:
Most roof inspections miss the subtle signs of trouble brewing in Central Texas homes. Our certified inspectors climb onto every plane (no drone shortcuts) to check for hail-induced cracks in south-facing shingles—the side that bears Austin's fiercest sun.

We examine attics for proper ventilation, a critical factor in preventing premature aging during our 100-degree streaks. For homes near the Balcones Fault, we look for stress cracks where limestone shifting has affected roof planes. Every report includes repair priority ratings so you know whether to act now or monitor.

Special attention goes to historic properties downtown, where we assess original materials like cedar shakes and terracotta tiles for restoration potential versus replacement needs. Newer subdivisions get hail resistance scoring based on their exposure in Williamson County's storm corridors.
process: Step 1: Exterior assessment of all roof surfaces and penetrations
Step 2: Attic inspection checking for moisture and ventilation issues
Step 3: Documentation with thermal imaging when temperature differentials allow
Step 4: Customized maintenance plan based on your roof type and age
benefits: Identifies small issues before they become expensive repairs
Prepares documentation for insurance renewals
Includes gutter and flashing condition reports
Provides realistic lifespan projections

faq.question: How often should I get my roof inspected in Austin?
faq.Answer: Annually—more frequently if you have an older roof or live in hail-prone areas like northern Williamson County where storms frequently track.

faq.question: Do you inspect tile roofs differently?
faq.Answer: Yes, we use specialized tools to check for cracked tiles under the surface and assess mortar conditions—common issues with Austin's freeze-thaw cycles.

services.name: Shingle Repair
services.shortDescription: Precision fixes that blend seamlessly with your existing roof.
services.description:
Those missing shingles after an Austin windstorm aren't just ugly—they're entry points for rain that can rot your decking. Our repair team carries color-matched architectural shingles for every major brand, ensuring patches disappear visually on your roof.

We go beyond simple replacements by addressing the root cause—whether it's inadequate nailing on new builds in Pflugerville or wind uplift from improper starter courses in Georgetown's older homes. Every repair includes surrounding shingle reinforcement to prevent future failures.

Special techniques preserve historic integrity for bungalows in Hyde Park, where we hand-cut replacement shingles to match original dimensions. For modern homes in Steiner Ranch, we ensure repairs maintain the energy-efficient reflective properties of your existing roof system.
process: Step 1: Damage assessment identifying underlying causes
Step 2: Material matching from our mobile inventory
Step 3: Precision removal without disturbing adjacent shingles
Step 4: Installation with sealant application at stress points
benefits: Same-day service for most minor repairs
Color blending for seamless aesthetics
Wind-resistant installation techniques
Warranty-backed workmanship

faq.question: Can you repair just a few missing shingles?
faq.Answer: Yes, but we'll advise if surrounding shingles show wear that makes spot repairs less effective—common in Austin homes over 15 years old.

faq.question: Will repairs affect my roof's warranty?
faq.Answer: Our work complies with manufacturer requirements when using approved materials. We document all repairs for your records.

services.name: Metal Roofing
services.shortDescription: Energy-efficient metal roofs built for Texas extremes.
services.description:
Austin's solar gain makes metal roofs an increasingly smart choice—their reflective surfaces can lower attic temperatures by 20°F compared to asphalt. We install standing seam systems that expand and contract silently during our 50-degree daily temperature swings.

For contemporary homes in West Austin, we offer sleek vertical panels with concealed fasteners. Hill Country estates get traditional stamped metal that mimics wood shake without the fire risk. Every installation includes specialized underlayment to prevent condensation—a must in our humid climate.

We engineer for Williamson County's unique challenges: reinforced seams for hail protection in Round Rock, and extra corrosion resistance for properties near Lake Travis where salty breezes accelerate wear. Commercial clients appreciate our 40-year finish warranties on panel systems.
process: Step 1: Structural evaluation ensuring your deck can support metal weight
Step 2: Custom fabrication with onsite measurements for perfect fit
Step 3: Installation with attention to thermal movement requirements
Step 4: Final inspection checking for proper water shedding
benefits: Class A fire rating for wildfire-prone areas
75% recycled content in most systems
Withstands 120 mph winds—critical for Austin's severe storms
Can be installed over existing roofs in many cases

faq.question: Are metal roofs noisy during rain?
faq.Answer: Not with proper installation—we include sound-dampening underlayment that makes rainfall quieter than on traditional shingles.

faq.question: How does metal perform in Austin hailstorms?
faq.Answer: Our panels resist denting from all but the largest hail, and we offer hail-resistant coatings that maintain warranty coverage after impacts.

serviceAreas.content:
Serving Round Rock since 2015, we understand how rapid growth has created roofing challenges in this bustling city. Many homes in master-planned communities like Teravista need specialized attention—their steep pitches require careful material handling during replacements. We're familiar with HOA requirements throughout the city and keep current on approved shingle colors for neighborhoods with strict aesthetic guidelines.

Our storm damage team frequently responds to hail claims along the I-35 corridor, where commercial properties like the Round Rock Premium Outlets need durable solutions. We coordinate with local insurance adjusters who know Williamson County's unique weather patterns.
serviceAreas.localContext: Family-oriented community with 1990s-2000s builds needing roof refreshes as original materials reach end-of-life.

serviceAreas.content:
Cedar Park's explosive growth means many roofs were installed during construction booms—some needing premature repairs. We specialize in identifying shortcuts taken by volume builders, particularly in subdivisions like Buttercup Creek where improper flashing installations are common.

The city's elevation makes it particularly vulnerable to high winds coming off the Hill Country. Our wind mitigation techniques include specialized hip and ridge cap installations that have protected homes through multiple storm seasons. We also service commercial properties along 183A, where flat roof systems require specialized maintenance.
serviceAreas.localContext: 2000s suburban expansion with modern homes needing weatherproofing upgrades.

serviceAreas.content:
Historic downtown Georgetown presents unique roofing challenges—from preserving century-old materials to matching modern replacements that meet strict preservation guidelines. We've restored slate roofs on Williamson County courthouse-area buildings using traditional techniques.

For newer developments like Santa Rita Ranch, we install impact-resistant materials suited for the area's frequent hail storms. Our team knows which neighborhoods are prone to clay soil shifting that can stress roof planes, and we design solutions accordingly. Georgetown's mix of old and new makes us particularly valuable here.
serviceAreas.localContext: Charming Victorian homes downtown contrast with rapidly expanding suburban developments.

serviceAreas.content:
Pflugerville's affordable housing boom means many roofs were built to minimum standards—we help homeowners upgrade before problems arise. The area's clay-heavy soil requires special attention to drainage systems that prevent foundation stresses from affecting roof integrity.

We service everything from first-time buyer homes in Falcon Pointe to custom builds near Lake Pflugerville. Our team keeps current on the city's rapid development, knowing which builders used quality materials versus those likely to need early replacement. Commercial clients along Pecan Street appreciate our fast response to storm damage.
serviceAreas.localContext: Fast-growing community with newer construction at various quality levels.

serviceAreas.content:
As Austin natives, we understand how drastically roofing needs vary across the city. Downtown high-rises need specialized access planning, while Barton Hills bungalows require careful historic preservation. We're experts in Austin's unique challenges—from UV degradation on west-facing slopes to proper ventilation for homes without attic space in central neighborhoods.

Our team services all major suburbs but remains deeply committed to central Austin, where we've restored roofs on everything from Sixth Street commercial buildings to Clarksville cottages. We navigate the city's complex permitting process efficiently and know which materials perform best in each microclimate.
serviceAreas.localContext: Eclectic mix from historic downtown properties to modern high-rises, all facing intense sun and storm exposure.

serviceAreas.content:
Leander's rapid northward expansion brings roofing challenges as new developments push into previously rural areas. We help homeowners transitioning from well-built older homes to newer constructions that may need quality upgrades. The area's exposure to northerly storms requires specialized wind-resistant installations.

Our commercial team services growing business parks along 183A, where flat roof systems must handle both heavy rains and reflective heat from adjacent parking lots. We're familiar with the city's evolving building codes and work closely with local inspectors to ensure compliance.
serviceAreas.localContext: Transitioning from small-town to suburban character with corresponding construction shifts.

serviceAreas.content:
Manor's agricultural roots mean we encounter unique roofing situations—from historic barn conversions to new subdivisions replacing farmland. We're experts in addressing the area's high wind exposure, particularly for homes near open fields where gusts accelerate.

The city's growth along US 290 brings commercial roofing opportunities, where we install durable systems for distribution centers and retail developments. For older homes in the downtown area, we specialize in matching original materials while improving weather resistance. Manor's mix of rural and urban makes our broad expertise particularly valuable here.
serviceAreas.localContext: Rural community transitioning to suburban with corresponding construction needs.

serviceAreas.content:
Liberty Hill's limestone-rich soil creates foundation movements that stress roofs—we design systems that accommodate slight shifts without compromising integrity. The area's rural character means many homes need specialized solutions like metal roofs that resist wildfire embers.

We service everything from historic downtown buildings to sprawling ranch properties, understanding how roofing needs differ across these property types. Our storm response team is particularly active here after northerly fronts bring high winds down the open plains. Commercial clients appreciate our knowledge of agricultural building requirements.
serviceAreas.localContext: Small-town atmosphere with increasing suburban development pressures.

serviceAreas.content:
Hutto's explosive growth means we're often repairing roofs installed during construction booms—identifying shortcuts taken by volume builders. The area's high clay content requires specialized attention to drainage systems that prevent foundation issues from affecting roof planes.

We service everything from starter homes in new developments to historic properties near Old Town. Our team knows which neighborhoods are prone to particular issues—like wind uplift in exposed subdivisions north of SH 79. Commercial clients along the 130 toll road appreciate our fast response to storm damage at industrial properties.
serviceAreas.localContext: Rapidly growing community with mix of affordable housing and custom builds.

serviceAreas.content:
Taylor's historic downtown presents unique roofing challenges—we've restored everything from 19th-century mercantile buildings to mid-century schools. The area's proximity to the Blackland Prairie means we design for both intense sun exposure and sudden downpours.

For newer developments near the Tesla gigafactory, we install commercial-grade systems that withstand industrial area conditions. Our team understands how the city's clay soils affect roof stresses differently than nearby sandy areas. Taylor's mix of old and new makes our broad expertise particularly valuable here.
serviceAreas.localContext: Historic charm meets rapid industrial growth with corresponding roofing needs.

serviceAreas.content:
West Lake Hills' luxury homes demand roofing solutions that match their high-end finishes. We specialize in premium materials like standing seam copper and slate alternatives that provide elegance without excessive maintenance. The area's steep slopes and dense tree cover require specialized safety protocols during installations.

Our team understands the neighborhood's strict aesthetic requirements and works closely with architects on custom solutions. We're experts in addressing the unique challenges of hillside homes—from proper drainage on sloping lots to wildfire-resistant materials for properties backing up to greenbelts.
serviceAreas.localContext: Affluent community with architecturally significant homes needing premium solutions.

serviceAreas.content:
Bee Cave's mix of luxury estates and commercial developments requires roofing expertise across multiple property types. We install high-end systems for Hill Country homes that withstand both intense sun and occasional freeze events. The area's elevation makes proper wind mitigation critical.

Our commercial team services retail centers along Highway 71, where we've designed energy-efficient systems for large footprint buildings. For residential clients, we're experts in matching materials to the area's rustic aesthetic while providing modern performance. Bee Cave's unique position between urban and rural makes our broad experience particularly valuable.
serviceAreas.localContext: Affluent community blending Hill Country character with upscale development.

serviceAreas.content:
Lakeway's lakeside location creates unique roofing challenges—from salt air corrosion to increased humidity under decks. We specialize in systems that withstand these conditions while maintaining the community's upscale aesthetic. The area's mature trees require careful attention to debris management in gutter systems.

Our team frequently works on homes in The Hills complex, understanding their specific material requirements. We're experts in addressing the reflection issues caused by Lake Travis, specifying UV-resistant materials for south-facing slopes. Commercial clients appreciate our knowledge of resort-style property needs along the 620 corridor.
serviceAreas.localContext: Luxury lakeside community with specific weather and aesthetic demands.

serviceAreas.content:
Dripping Springs' rural character means we encounter everything from historic farmhouses to modern vineyard estates. We specialize in fire-resistant materials for properties backing up to wildland areas—a growing concern in our changing climate. The area's limestone bedrock requires specialized flashing techniques where roofs meet stone walls.

Our team understands how to blend traditional aesthetics with modern performance, whether restoring a century-old barn roof or installing a new metal system on a Hill Country mansion. Commercial clients along US 290 appreciate our knowledge of agricultural building requirements and tasting room design needs.
serviceAreas.localContext: Hill Country community balancing rural preservation with growth pressures.

reviews.name: James T.
reviews.text: After the April hailstorm left my Round Rock home with dozens of damaged shingles, Apex had a crew out within hours to tarp the worst spots. Their detailed documentation helped State Farm approve a full replacement without hassle. The new impact-resistant shingles look great and give me peace of mind.

reviews.name: Maria G.
reviews.text: We chose Apex for our Barton Hills bungalow's metal roof after seeing their work on a neighbor's historic home. They perfectly matched the standing seam profile to maintain our 1940s aesthetic while adding modern weather protection. Even during heavy rains, the roof is whisper quiet.

reviews.name: David & Sarah L.
reviews.text: Our Pflugerville home's original roof was failing after just 12 years—turns out the builder used cheap materials. Apex explained all our options clearly and installed a premium system that should last decades. They even coordinated with our HOA to ensure color compliance.

reviews.name: Robert K.
reviews.text: As property manager for a Georgetown retail center, I appreciate Apex's proactive approach. Their semi-annual inspections caught minor flashing issues before they became leaks. When we needed a section replaced after a storm, they worked nights to avoid disrupting tenants.

reviews.name: Lisa M.
reviews.text: Our Cedar Park home's original gutters couldn't handle Austin's downpours. Apex designed a custom 6" system with extra downspouts that solved our foundation drainage issues. Two years later, not a single overflow—even during the Memorial Day floods.

reviews.name: Carlos R.
reviews.text: The free inspection revealed ventilation problems in our Mueller attic that were cooking our shingles from underneath. Apex's affordable fix added years to our roof's life. Their thermal imaging showed exactly where heat was trapped—super insightful!

reviews.name: Emily S.
reviews.text: When wind tore shingles off our East Austin bungalow, Apex matched the vintage-style architectural shingles perfectly. The repair blends seamlessly—you'd never know it wasn't original. Their crew even repaired our historic wood soffit at no extra charge.
